perturbation.original_text: Clause 1 Confidential Information - information of whatever kind and in whatever form contained (and includes in particular but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, documents, drawings, computerized information, films, tapes, specifications, designs, models, equipment or data of any kind) which is clearly identified by the Disclosing Party as confidential by an appropriate legend or if orally disclosed then upon disclosure or within 30 days of such oral disclosure identified in writing by the Disclosing Party as confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 1 Confidential Information - information of whatever kind and in whatever form contained (and includes in particular but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, documents, drawings, computerized information, films, tapes, specifications, designs, models, equipment or data of any kind) which is considered confidential by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of 'Confidential Information' to remove the explicit requirement for clear identification (legend or written notice within 30 days for oral disclosures) introduces ambiguity. Norwegian law may have specific requirements for what constitutes legally protected confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original text mandates clear identification of confidential information. The modified text makes it subjectively 'considered confidential' which is open to interpretation and potentially contradicts the Trade Secrets Act's requirement for demonstrable effort to maintain secrecy, which implicit agreement doesn't satisfy.

Р о з д і л I ЗАГАЛЬНІ ПОЛОЖЕННЯ Стаття 1. Визначення термінів 1. Для цілей цього Закону вживаються такі терміни: аудіовізуальна інформація - будь-які сигнали, що сприймаються зоровими і слуховими рецепторами людини та ідентифікуються як повідомлення про події, факти, явища, процеси, відомості про осіб, а також коментарі (думки) про них, що передаються за доп
perturbation.justification: The original contract requires 'Confidential Information' to be clearly identified via a legend or written notice. The changed text removes this explicit requirement, stating information is simply 'considered confidential'. The Law Explanation states that this introduces ambiguity and potentially contradicts the Trade Secrets Act's requirement for demonstrable effort to maintain secrecy. While the scraped snippets do not explicitly detail these Norwegian laws, the Law Explanation's reference to the Trade Secrets Act, combined with the removal of explicit identification requirements, suggests a weakening of the protection of trade secrets and a move towards a more subjective standard, making it harder to enforce confidentiality and therefore creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes;
perturbation.changed_text: b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for any purpose it deems necessary;
perturbation.explanation: Changing the permitted use of confidential information from 'LOA purposes' to 'any purpose it deems necessary' significantly broadens the scope of permissible use and contradicts the core principle of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Norwegian Contract Law principles related to good faith and fair dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Norwegian contract law, although not codified, relies on principles of good faith and fair dealing. Allowing the Receiving Party to use confidential information for 'any purpose it deems necessary' allows them to act in bad faith, thus, going against Norwegian Contract Law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Numerical studies of a plasma diode with external forcing View Item Home Øvrige samlinger Høstingsarkiver CRIStin høstingsarkiv View Item Home Øvrige samlinger Høstingsarkiver CRIStin høstingsarkiv View Item JavaScript is disabled for your browser. Some features of this site may not work without it. Numerical studies of a plasma diode with external forcing Rekaa, Vegard Lundby ; Pecseli, Hans ; Trulsen, Jan Karsten Journal article ; PublishedVersion ; Peer reviewed View/ Open 1-2E4747620.pdf (4.201Mb) Year 2012 Permanent link http://urn.nb.no/ URN:NBN:no-61646 CRIStin 969112 Metadata Show metadata Appears in the following Collection Fysisk institutt [3988] Institutt for teoretisk astrofysikk [1481] CRIStin høstingsarkiv [35810] Original version Physics of Plasmas. 2012, 19 (8):082115, DOI: http://dx.doi.org/10.1063/1.4747620 Abstract With reference to laboratory Q-machine studies we analyze the dynamics of a plasma diode under external forcing. Assuming a strong axial magnetic field, the problem is analyzed in one spatial dimension by a particle-in-cell code. The cathode is assumed to be operated in electron rich conditions, supplying an abundance of electrons. We compare different forcing schemes with the results obtained by solving the van der Pol equation. In one method of forcing we apply an oscillation in addition to the DC end plate bias and consider both amplitude and frequency variations. An alternative method of perturbation consists of modelling an absorbing grid at some internal position. Also in this case we can have a constant frequency with varying amplitude or alternatively an oscillation with chirped frequency but constant amplitude. We find that the overall features of the forced van der Pol equation are recovered, but the details in the plasma response need more attention to the harmonic responses, requiring extensions of the model equation. The analysis is extended by introducing collisional effects, where we emphasize charge exchange collisions of ions, since these processes usually have the largest cross sections and give significant modifications of the diode performance. In particular we find a reduction in oscillator frequency, although a linear scaling of the oscillation time with the system length remains also in this case. This article may be downloaded for personal use only. Any other use requires prior permission of the author and AIP Publishing. perturbation.justification: The original contract restricts the use of confidential information to 'LOA purposes,' while the changed text broadens this to 'any purpose it deems necessary.' This change directly contradicts the core principle of confidentiality by allowing the Receiving Party to use confidential information for potentially harmful purposes, according to the Perturbation Explanation. The Law Explanation states that this change allows the Receiving Party to act in bad faith, conflicting with principles of good faith and fair dealing in Norwegian contract law. While the scraped snippets are unhelpful, the Perturbation and Law Explanations clearly show that the change significantly weakens the obligation to protect confidential information and introduces the potential for misuse.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall, upon 30 days written request by the other Party, return to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof together with a confirmation in writing that the Confidential Information in computerized form has been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 5 Either Party shall, return, if they so choose, to the other party all Confidential Information which the latter has disclosed to the former, together with all copies or reproductions thereof together with a confirmation in writing that the Confidential Information in computerized form has been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the mandatory 'upon 30 days written request' and replacing it with a discretionary 'if they so choose' undermines the obligation to return confidential information, potentially conflicting with legal requirements for protecting trade secrets and confidential data.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified clause makes the return of information discretionary, contradicting the requirement to actively protect confidential information to maintain its trade secret status. Section 5 of the Norwegian Trade Secrets Act implicitly requires reasonable measures to prevent unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of trade secrets, which is not satisfied with the modified term.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Lov om endringer i straffeprosessloven og tvisteloven mv. (fjernmøter og fjernavhør i domstolene mv.) - Lovdata Til hovedinnhold Verktøylinje Lov om endringer i straffeprosessloven og tvisteloven mv. (fjernmøter og fjernavhør i domstolene mv.)  ⎙  ⎙ Skriv ut  Del dokument Opprinnelig kunngjort versjon Opprinnelig kunngjort versjon PDF-versjon Signatur Lov om endringer i straffeprosessloven og tvisteloven mv. (fjernmøter og fjernavhør i domstolene mv.) Dato LOV-2022-06-17-58 Departement Justis- og beredskapsdepartementet Ikrafttredelse Kongen bestemmer, 01.01.2024 Endrer LOV-1915-08-13-5 , LOV-1981-05-22-25 , LOV-2005-06-17-90 , LOV-2013-06-21-100 , LOV-2014-06-20-49 Kunngjort 17.06.2022 kl. 12.35 Journalnr 2022-0738 Korttittel Endringslov til straffeprosessloven og tvisteloven mv. Prop.97 L (2021–2022) , Innst.384 L (2021–2022) , Lovvedtak 86 (2021–2022) . Stortingets første og andre gangs behandling hhv. 3. og 7. juni 2022. Fremmet av Justis- og beredskapsdepartementet. Endringer i følgende lover: 1 Lov 13. august 1915 nr. 5 om domstolene (domstolloven) . 2 Lov 22. mai 1981 nr. 25 om rettergangsmåten i straffesaker (Straffeprosessloven) . 3 Lov 17. juni 2005 nr. 90 om mekling og rettergang i sivile tvister (tvisteloven) . 4 Lov 21. juni 2013 nr. 100 om fastsetjing og endring av eigedoms- og rettshøve på fast eigedom m.m. (jordskiftelova) . 5 Lov 20. juni 2014 nr. 49 om konfliktrådsbehandling (konfliktrådsloven) . I I lov 13. august 1915 nr. 5 om domstolene skal ny § 124 a lyde: § 124 a. Retten kan beslutte offentlig overføring av lyd og bilde fra et rettsmøte (strømming) så langt det ikke gjelder begrensninger i offentligheten for rettsmøtet og hensynet til personvern eller andre hensyn ikke taler avgjørende imot. Partene skal ha anledning til å uttale seg før retten treffer sin beslutning. Er det oppnevnt bistandsadvokat i saken, skal også bistandsadvokaten få uttale seg om spørsmålet. Personopplysningsloven gjelder for domstolens behandling av personopplysninger i forbindelse med strømming etter første ledd. Domstolen kan behandle personopplysninger, herunder personopplysninger som nevnt i personvernforordningen artikkel 9 og 10 , når det er nødvendig for å utføre oppgaver i forbindelse med strømming etter første ledd. Domstolen kan blant annet lagre opptak av overføringen. Departementet kan gi forskrift om strømming etter første ledd, blant annet om behandling av personopplysninger. II I lov 22. mai 1981 nr. 25 om rettergangsmåten i straffesaker gjøres følgende endringer: Kapittel 4 overskriften skal lyde: Kap 4. Rettsbøker og rettsmøter. Ny §
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates the return of confidential information upon a 30-day written request. The changed contract replaces this mandatory obligation with a discretionary "if they so choose," which fundamentally undermines the original clause's intent. The Law Explanation posits that this change contradicts the requirement to actively protect confidential information. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 doesn't directly address trade secrets, the Perturbation Explanation correctly identifies that altering the obligation to return confidential information could conflict with legal requirements for protecting trade secrets and confidential data. The change introduces a significant weakening of the obligation, making compliance uncertain and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 14 Any disputes between the Parties arising out of or in connection with the NDA and which cannot be settled amicably shall be settled in the common court of law. The legal venue is Oslo tingrett, Norway.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 14 Any disputes between the Parties arising out of or in connection with the NDA and which cannot be settled amicably shall be settled out of court.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the dispute resolution mechanism from the 'common court of law' to an unspecified 'out of court' settlement method removes the guaranteed legal recourse and could violate fundamental principles of access to justice.
perturbation.law_explanation: Norwegian Constitution § 92 guarantees the right to have claims tried before a court of law. By changing the term to 'settled out of court', the text violates this section.
perturbation.justification: The original contract text specifies disputes will be settled in the "common court of law", ensuring access to the legal system. The changed text alters this by stating disputes will be "settled out of court." This removes the legally guaranteed recourse and therefore contradicts the Norwegian Constitution § 92, according to the Law Explanation, which guarantees the right to have claims tried before a court of law. While the scraped snippets are not helpful, the Law Explanation clearly explains the contradiction.
